content.problemStatement:
Chef has two strings AAA and BBB consisting of lowercase alphabets, each of length NNN. Help Chef in finding the number of indices iii (1≤i≤N)(1 \leq i \leq N)(1≤i≤N) such that A[i…N]<B[i…N]A[i \dots N] \lt B[i \dots N]A[i…N]<B[i…N].


S[i…N]S[i \dots N]S[i…N] denotes the suffix of string SSS starting at index iii, i.e. SiSi+1S_iS_{i+1}Si​Si+1​ …\dots… SNS_{N}SN​.
String S<S \ltS< String TTT denotes that SSS is lexicographically smaller than TTT. If two strings SSS and TTT have the same length NNN, we say that SSS is lexicographically smaller than TTT if there exists an index i(1≤i≤N)i(1 \le i \le N)i(1≤i≤N) such that S1=T1,S_1 = T_1,S1​=T1​, S2=T2,…,Si−1=Ti−1S_2 = T_2, \ldots, S_{i-1} = T_{i-1}S2​=T2​,…,Si−1​=Ti−1​ and Si<TiS_i \lt T_iSi​<Ti​. For example, "abcabcabc" is lexicographically smaller than "acdacdacd", "abeabeabe", but not smaller than "abcabcabc", "aacaacaac".

Input Format

The first line contains TTT denoting the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains NNN, denoting the length of the strings AAA and BBB.
The second line of each test case contains the string AAA.
The third line of each test case contains the string BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer -  the number of indices iii such that A[i…N]<B[i…N]A[i \dots N] \lt B[i\dots N]A[i…N]<B[i…N].
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^6
A and B consist only of lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- A line with integer N, the length of the strings.
- A line with string A of length N (lowercase letters).
- A line with string B of length N (lowercase letters).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one integer – the number of indices i (1 ≤ i ≤ N) such that the suffix A[i…N] is lexicographically smaller than the suffix B[i…N].

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: suffixes at i=1 give "ab" < "bb" (true), i=2 gives "b" = "b" (false). Answer = 1.\nTest case 2: all three suffixes of A are lexicographically smaller than the corresponding suffixes of B, so answer = 3.
Every suffix of A starts with 'z' which is greater than the corresponding suffix of B starting with 'a', therefore no index satisfies the condition.

hidden.label: mixed case with two valid indices

solution.approach:
Traverse the strings from right to left. Let dp[i] be true if A[i…N] < B[i…N].
- If A[i] < B[i] then dp[i] = true.
- If A[i] > B[i] then dp[i] = false.
- If A[i] == B[i] then dp[i] = dp[i+1] (the result depends on the next position).
Initialize dp[N+1] = false because two empty strings are equal, not smaller. While scanning, keep a running sum of dp[i] to obtain the answer. This runs in O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the strings. Over all test cases the total time is O(ΣN) ≤ 10^6.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space (besides the input strings).

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    it = iter(data)
    t = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(next(it))
        a = next(it).strip()
        b = next(it).strip()
        ans = 0
        next_less = 0  # dp[i+1]
        for i in range(n-1, -1, -1):
            if a[i] < b[i]:
                next_less = 1
            elif a[i] > b[i]:
                next_less = 0
            # else keep previous
            ans += next_less
        out_lines.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
